title: zea mays subsp. indurata
intro: Zea mays subsp. indurata, also known as Tohono O'odham Purple Corn, is a subspecies of maize that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its rich, deep purple color and high antioxidant content, and has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Zea mays subsp. indurata include its ability to help reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against cell damage. Tohono O'odham Purple Corn is typically harvested and prepared as a herbal tea, porridge, or flour, which can be used to make a variety of traditional dishes and remedies.
